key_insight: Pt nanoislands on TiO2 are not just passive cocatalysts: isolated ~10 nm Pt islands show strong, broadband visible-light photocatalysis with ~1% internal quantum efficiency across the visible range.
engineering_relevance: This suggests catalytic metals with damped optics can be engineered as active light absorbers in metal-semiconductor devices, enabling new design rules for visible-light photocatalysts and optoelectronic junctions.
method: Quantitative scanning photoelectrochemical microscopy to map wavelength-resolved EQE.
Comparison of Au and Pt nanoisland arrays on TiO2 across visible wavelengths.
Correlation of photocatalytic activity with island morphology and extinction spectra.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports Pt EQE per atom is ~20× higher than Au at 455 nm and ~2× higher at 595 nm, with Pt showing ~1% IQE that is roughly wavelength-independent across the visible.
watch_item: Pt photoactivity drops strongly when nanoislands become connected, so performance depends sensitively on nanoscale morphology and percolation state.
paper_title: Platinum is a Photocatalyst: Large Visible-Light Quantum Efficiency Revealed
